name: v05-parallel-foreach-with-wait
description: v0.6 Phase 3 (second half) demo — parallel forEach with a wait inside the iteration body. The handler fans out over `req.body.items` with `mode: parallel` + `concurrency: 5`. Each iteration body records its item then waits 100ms before the next iteration step (the wait simulates per-item throttle or coordination delay). When the FIRST iteration's wait fires, the runner persists a parallel-mode iteration cursor with `waitFiringIteration`, sparse `completedResults`, and explicit `cancelledIterations`. Peer iterations get cancelled via a pool AbortController (distinct from `ctx.signal` so user-cancel doesn't get confused with peer-wait cancel). On dispatchDeferred re-entry, ForEachNode pre-populates `results[]` from `completedResults`, re-launches cancelled iterations from inner step 0, and resumes the wait-firing iteration at its `innerStepIndex`. See `docs/c/devtools/parallel-foreach-wait-spec.mdx` for the full design. Smoke validates the first 202 + Location header; full round-trip in vitest.

inputs.expression: ({ item: ctx.state.item, index: ctx.state.itemIndex, recordedAt: Date.now() })
